item.title: All NASA News - NASA
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.

item.title: NASA Media Contacts - NASA

item.title: NASA News
item.description: Resources for news media, including NASA news contacts and archived news material

item.title: NASA Armstrong Director Brad Flick to Retire After 40 Years of Service - NASA
item.description: On Monday, NASA announced Bradley Flick, director of NASA’s Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, will retire Thursday, March 19, after a

item.title: NASA to Share Artemis II Flight Readiness Review Update - NASA
item.description: NASA will host a news conference at 3 p.m. EDT, Thursday, March 12, to highlight progress toward the Artemis II crewed mission around the Moon. The media

item.title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Students in New York - NASA
item.description: Students in New York will hear from NASA astronauts Jack Hathaway and Chris Williams as they answer prerecorded science, technology, engineering, and

item.title: 2026 News Releases - NASA
item.description: Sign up to receive NASA news releases and other information.

item.title: Spacewalk Preps and Health Checks Using Augmented Reality, Artificial Intelligence - NASA
item.description: Spacewalk preparations are underway aboard the International Space Station as two astronauts check their spacesuits and review procedures. The Expedition 74 crew also experimented with augmented reality and artificial intelligence to conduct health checks in space. Meanwhile, a U.S. cargo spacecraft nears it departure this week.

item.title: NASA’s DART Mission Changed Orbit of Asteroid Didymos Around Sun
item.description: Discover how NASA’s DART mission moved beyond its initial goal, shifting the entire Didymos asteroid system’s orbit around the sun. Learn how this breakthrough proves humanity's ability to protect Earth through planetary defense.

item.title: US-French Satellite Takes Stock of World’s River Water
item.description: Rivers rise and fall throughout the year, but by how much? Perhaps less than previously thought, according to new data from the SWOT mission. Hidden riverbed contours are also emerging.

item.title: NASA’s Curiosity Rover Sees Martian ‘Spiderwebs’ Up Close
item.description: A hilly landscape that looks like spiderwebs when viewed from orbit holds clues to the history of water on ancient Mars.

item.title: NASA’s Perseverance Now Autonomously Pinpoints Its Location on Mars
item.description: Using the new Mars Global Localization algorithm, NASA’s Perseverance rover can now pinpoint its exact location without help from engineers on Earth.

item.title: Archival Data From NASA’s NEOWISE Tracks Star Turning Into Black Hole
item.description: Using archival data from the NEOWISE mission, astronomers have captured the rare moment a massive star skipped the supernova phase and collapsed directly into a black hole.

item.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Completes First AI-Planned Drive on Mars
item.description: In a first for planetary exploration, NASA utilized generative AI to map routes for the Perseverance rover, cutting planning time and increasing science returns.

item.title: NASA-ISRO Radar Mission Peers Through Clouds to See Mississippi River Delta
item.description: NASA’s NISAR mission reveals new details of the Mississippi River Delta’s forests and wetlands, proving radar’s power to monitor ecosystems regardless of weather.

item.title: NASA Analysis Shows La Niña Limited Sea Level Rise in 2025
item.description: A mild La Niña caused greater rainfall over the Amazon basin, which offset rising sea levels due to record warming of Earth’s oceans.

item.title: NASA, SpaceX Launch US-European Satellite to Monitor Earth’s Oceans
item.description: Sentinel-6B will extend a decades-long legacy of global sea level measurements that help forecast weather, protect coastal communities, and more.

item.title: News
item.description: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, the leading center for robotic exploration of the solar system.

item.title: February 2026 Company Update
item.description: February marked another month of critical progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released an additional 1,826 flight components. The stage two shipping assembly and stage two fluids top-level assembly are now fully released, enabling final integration activit

item.title: January 2026 Company Update
item.description: January marked a strong start to the year, with meaningful progress across design, build, test, and launch. Design This month, the team released 1,856 flight parts. At this stage of the program, part releases are primarily for secondary structures and integration hardware that will be installed l

item.title: December 2025 Company Update
item.description: As we start the new year, here’s a look at key Terran R program milestones from December 2025.  Design This month, the team released 1,526 flight parts and the integrated thrust structure fluids assembly, unlocking downstream manufacturing and keeping the integration schedule moving at p

item.title: November 2025 Company Update
item.description: Throughout November, the Terran R program made key advancements across design, build, test, and launch operations. Design As the program continues to mature, we are releasing larger groups of parts through our design sprints. In November, we released 1,602 parts, unlocking downstream manufacturin
